title: Animal Habitats
description: Learn about where and how animals live, how they play, and how we can help them. When you've earned this badge, you'll know more about wild animals and how to protect their homes.

title: Automotive 1: Design
description: Do you want to take a ride in your dream car? Before you can do that, you need to design it! Discover how to create vehicles by sketching and sculpting a model. When you've earned this badge, you'll know about innovation and market research. You'll know how to create criteria, sketch, and sculpt a vehicle.

requirements.title: Sketch a vehicle to meet your criteria

title: Automotive 2: Engineering
description: Find out how automotive engineers fuel the future by building and testing vehicles. Then, create your own model of an alternative fuel vehicle! When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to use the Design Thinking Process to plan, build, test, and improve prototypes.

requirements.title: Learn about simple machines in vehicles
Engineer a vehicle that uses alternative fuel
Test and revise your vehicle prototype
Share your vehicle prototype and testing results

title: Automotive 3: Manufacturing
description: Explore how automotive manufacturers build lots of vehicles without wasting time and how they make sure the vehicles are ready for the road! When you've earned this badge, you'll know how high quality vehicles are manufactured.

requirements.title: Learn about the automotive manufacturing process
Plan your own automotive manufacturing process

title: Budget Maker
description: Create a budget to help you plan to meet your goals and learn smart ways to spend, save, and share money. When you've earned this badge, you'll be able to create a budget to save for the things you want and need.

requirements.title: Investigate why you want what you want
Learn how to decide what to buy
Find out how financial services help people save

title: Business Jumpstart
description: Everyone has the ability to come up with an idea-it's what you do to bring it to life that makes you an entrepreneur! When you've earned this badge, you will have come up with an idea, gotten feedback on it, and pitched it to others-just like an entrepreneur!

requirements.title: Create a prototype that solves a problem
Pitch your idea to an audience

title: Camper
description: Find out how to plan a camping adventure. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to have a great overnight camping trip at a campsite or cabin. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Jeweler, Gardener, Detective, Camper, and Independence badges.

requirements.title: Head out on your trip-and have some nighttime fun!

title: Coding for Good 1: Coding Basics
description: Find out how programmers write computer programs for computers to solve problems. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how programmers write computer programs and how people can use computers to help others.

requirements.title: Create algorithms for a computer that follow a sequence
Use loops to improve your algorithm
Keep your code interesting with conditionals
Create your own set of commands that use conditionals
Learn about women in computer science

title: Coding for Good 2: Digital Game Design
description: Find out how programmers create a video game that is fun and helps solve a problem. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to use iteration to plan, build, and test a game "for good."

requirements.title: Discover how game design can be used "for good"
Explore tools used to develop digital games
Build, test, and improve your maze game using iteration

title: Coding for Good 3: App Development
description: Design your own idea for an app that solves a problem. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to use user-centered design to create an app.

requirements.title: Decompose the needs of your app user
Include conditionals in your app design
Share and improve your app with user feedback

title: Cookie Collaborator
description: Learn to collaborate with your troop, community, and network to grow your cookie business. When you've earned this badge, you will know how to work with your Girl Scout team to set goals and make decisions to run your cookie business, and how to collaborate with your friends and family to connect with more customers.

requirements.title: Get to know the Girl Scout Cookie business

title: Cookie Entrepreneur Family Pin
description: Have fun with your family as you earn this award while running your Girl Scout Cookie® business.

title: Cookie Entrepreneur Family Pin
description: Have fun with your family as you earn this award while running your Girl Scout Cookie® business.

title: Cybersecurity 1: Basics
description: Find out how computers talk to each other. When you've earned this badge, you'll know the basics of cybersecurity and how computers communicate.

requirements.title: Find out how computers read information
Find out what protocols are and create one

title: Cybersecurity 2: Safeguards
description: Find out how to keep your online identity safe. When you've earned this badge, you'll know know how to protect your online identity and stay safe online.

requirements.title: Discover how you share information and what to share
Find out how information online can last forever
Find out who is trustworthy online

title: Cybersecurity 3: Investigator
description: Become a cybersecurity investigator and learn how to spot threats online. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how computers use codes to communicate and how to spot cyber crime.

requirements.title: Create and crack a shift cipher code
Find out how updates can help your security
Find out what to do if your identity is stolen
Investigate if a message is real or fake

title: Democracy for Juniors
description: Learn more about how your government works-from your town or city to the whole country. When you've earned this badge, you will know more about how government works- from your town or city to the whole country.

requirements.title: Find out about our country's legislative branch
Find out about our country's executive branch
Find out about our country's judicial branch

title: Detective
description: Try out the skills that make great detectives. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to follow clues and discover facts like a real detective. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Jeweler, Gardener, Detective, Camper, and Independence badges.

requirements.title: Follow the clues to solve a real mystery!

title: Digital Leadership
description: Find out how you can use technology to help others and explore how to learn, share, and lead in the digital world. When you've earned this badge, you will know how to protect yourself and others online. You'll know how technology can make the world a better place.

requirements.title: Explore how you connect with others

title: Digital Photographer
description: Find out how to capture a winning photo with your digital device, whether you're using a phone, tablet, laptop, or digital camera. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to use a digital camera to create one- of-a-kind photos. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Digital Photographer, Staying Fit, Musician, Entertainment Technology, and Scribe badges.

requirements.title: Learn about digital cameras from an expert

title: Eco Camper
description: Find out how to protect the environment when you go on a camping trip. When you've earned this badge, you'll have learned skills for minimal impact camping and know how to protect the environment when you go outdoors.

requirements.title: Learn the Leave No Trace Seven Principles
Plan meals with the environment in mind
Have fun with Leave No Trace

title: Entertainment Technology
description: Explore the ins and outs of entertainment technology. When you've earned this badge, you'll know the science behind the world of entertainment. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Digital Photographer, Staying Fit, Musician, Entertainment Technology, and Scribe badges.

requirements.title: Try the science of amusement park rides

title: Flowers
description: Discover that there's more to flowers than just good looks. When you've earned this badge, you'll know all that's wonderful about flowers.

requirements.title: Uncover the science of one flower
Find out how flowers help people
Send a message in flower code

title: Gardener
description: Find out how to make your own garden, no matter where you live. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to help plants and flowers grow. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Jeweler, Gardener, Detective, Camper, and Independence badges.

requirements.title: Learn how to choose garden plants

title: Geocacher
description: Find out about geocaching. You'll search for treasure chests, called "geocaches," using a device called a GPS receiver. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to have a geocaching adventure. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Geocacher, Animal Habitats, Playing the Past, Product Designer, and Social Butterfly badges.

requirements.title: Learn to use a GPS receiver
Take part in a bug's travels!

title: Horseback Riding
description: Find out about horse behavior and riding basics. When you've earned this badge, you'll know what it takes to be a good rider-from understanding horse body language to controlling the horse's speed while you're riding.

requirements.title: Know the basics of horseback riding
Take care of a horse and learn about safety

title: Independence
description: Get to know yourself and what you're capable of so you can feel confident on your own. When you've earned this badge, you'll feel more confident doing things on your own- and know how to help others be confident in you. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Jeweler, Gardener, Detective, Camper, and Independence badges.

title: Inside Government
description: Find out more about the basics of government and start to become an active citizen. When you've earned this badge, you'll know more about how government is run.

requirements.title: Decide what being an active citizen means to you

title: Journey: Agent of Change
description: In this Journey, you will:

requirements.title: Explore how powerful individual women have been throughout history, how your own individual powers can be linked together to create a powerful team, and how your team power can become community power.
Plan a Take Action project that helps others.

Earn 3 leadership awards: Power of One, Power of Team, and Power of Community. If you're a Girl Scout volunteer, go to Volunteer Toolkit for complete meeting plans and activity instructions. Learn more about how to earn your Take Action Award - and help your community - with the Girl Scout Take Action Guide. Then use your leadership skills to earn your Bronze Award, the highest award for Girl Scout Juniors!

title: Journey: GET MOVING!
description: In this Journey, you will:

requirements.title: Explore energy and how to use it wisely by interviewing power-use experts and conducting an energy audits of a building in your community.
Plan a Take Action project to fix an energy problem in your community. Your could launch carpools, work to dim the lights on city buildings, or promote energy savings at your school.

Earn 3 leadership awards: The Energize Award, the Investigate Award, and the Innovate Award. If you're a Girl Scout volunteer, go to Volunteer Toolkit for complete meeting plans and activity instructions.

title: Journey: Outdoor (Junior)
description: In this Journey, you will:

requirements.title: Deepen your outdoor skills when you earn your Animal Habitat, Camper, and Eco Camper badges.
Plan a Take Action project that helps make your favorite park, beach, or forest a better place for everyone.

Earn a Take Action Award. If you're a Girl Scout volunteer, go to Volunteer Toolkit for complete meeting plans and activity instructions. Learn more about how to earn your Take Action Award - and help your community - with the Girl Scout Take Action Guide. Then use your leadership skills to earn your Bronze Award, the highest award for Girl Scout Juniors!

title: Journey: Think Like a Citizen Scientist
description: In this Journey, you will:

requirements.title: Find out how citizen scientists make observations, collect data, and work with scientists to receive feedback on research.
Do 3 citizen science activities: sharpen your observation skills through 2 observation games and a SciStarter project.

Plan a Take Action project that helps others. If you're a Girl Scout volunteer, go to Volunteer Toolkit for complete meeting plans and activity instructions. Learn more about how to earn your Take Action Award - and help your community - with the Girl Scout Take Action Guide. Then use your leadership skills to earn your Bronze Award, the highest award for Girl Scout Juniors!

title: Journey: Think Like a Programmer
description: In this Journey, you will:

requirements.title: Find out how programmers use computational thinking to solve problems.

Do 3 computational thinking activities: create algorithms to make images with tangrams; create mad libs and craft suncatchers to learn more about algorithms, abstraction, functions, and variables; and create a personal innovation to discover rapid prototyping.

Plan a Take Action project that helps others. If you're a Girl Scout volunteer, go to Volunteer Toolkit for complete meeting plans and activity instructions. Learn more about how to earn your Take Action Award - and help your community - with the Girl Scout Take Action Guide. Then use your leadership skills to earn your Bronze Award, the highest award for Girl Scout Juniors!

title: Journey: Think Like an Engineer
description: In this Journey, you will:

requirements.title: Find out how engineers use design thinking to solve problems

Do 3 design thinking activities: design and build a paper structure that can support the weight of heavy books, an emergency shelter, and a prototype of a structure that can withstand an earthquake's shaking.

Plan a Take Action project that helps others. If you're a Girl Scout volunteer, go to Volunteer Toolkit for complete meeting plans and activity instructions. Learn more about how to earn your Take Action Award - and help your community - with the Girl Scout Take Action Guide. Then use your leadership skills to earn your Bronze Award, the highest award for Girl Scout Juniors!

title: Journey: aMUSE
description: In this Journey, you will:

requirements.title: Explore roles you play in your life and try on new ones-play a role-playing game, invite actors to talk about the characters they've played, or learn to spot stereotypes on TV, in movies, or in ads.

Plan a Take Action Project, such as putting on a performance that creatively urges an end to stereotyping, drawing a graphic novel to share with younger girls, or starting a "mix it up" day in the school cafeteria and having girls sit with new people. More Details

title: Junior Art and Design
description: Examine and explore different styles and elements of art and design. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to turn ideas into art and design projects.

requirements.title: Discover art and design around you

title: Junior Craft and Tinker
description: Learn how to make something with care, skill, and imagination and then improve it. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to craft and tinker to solve problems.

requirements.title: Craft for form, fit, and function

title: Junior Create and Innovate
description: Explore how makers use their imaginations to solve problems, build new things, and change the world. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to use the design thinking process to improve things you create for others.

title: Junior First Aid
description: Find out how to help people when they're ill or injured and learn how to respond during an emergency. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to help people who are sick or hurt.

requirements.title: Learn the first steps to take in an emergency
Find out how to handle urgent first-aid issues
Know how to take care of someone who's sick

title: Junior Girl Scout Way
description: Find out about Girl Scout traditions and share activities and spirit that are a century strong. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to use the Girl Scout ways and traditions to make the world a better place.

requirements.title: Leave a place better than you found it

title: Junior STEM Career Exploration
description: Explore what you're interested in and what you care about. Then, create a plan for how you'll change the world! When you've earned this badge, you'll know about STEM careers that make the world a better place.

title: Junior Snow or Climbing Adventure
description: Choose your adventure: Go downhill skiing/snowboarding, or recreational tree climbing. When you've earned this badge, you'll have learned the skills-and experienced- downhill skiing, snowboarding, or recreational tree climbing.

requirements.title: Set a goal and train for your adventure

title: Junior Trail Adventure
description: Choose your adventure: Go on a trail run for a distance that challenges you, or train for and go on three separate day hikes. When you've earned this badge, you'll have planned and learned the skills for trail running or three separate day hikes.

requirements.title: Set a goal and train for your adventure

title: Math in Nature 1: Shapes in Nature
description: Explore patterns found in nature and use math to create your own. When you've earned this badge, you will know about symmetry, fractals, and the Fibonacci sequence. You'll have identified and made patterns inspired by nature.

requirements.title: Create nature-inspired art with circular symmetry

title: Math in Nature 2: Numbers in Nature
description: Explore how math can be used to tell us all kinds of information about nature, from how tall or old a tree is to the weather, season, or time. When you've earned this badge, you will know how to use math to tell time, predict the weather, and learn about trees. You'll know how to measure shadows, perimeter, and area.

requirements.title: Find the area and perimeter of plants

title: Math in Nature 3: Design with Nature
description: Use math to plan and organize an outdoor adventure. Decide where you'll go, how long you'll be gone for, and what you'll bring. When you've earned this badge, you will know about different types of maps. You'll know how to calculate distance, pace, elevation changes, and area.

requirements.title: Find changes in elevation on a map
Decide how much food to bring

title: Mechanical Engineering: Balloon Car
description: Learn about air power and create an alternative fuel car. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to engineer a balloon-powered car and understand potential energy, kinetic energy, and jet propulsion.

requirements.title: Learn about potential and kinetic energy
Design and build a balloon car
Brainstorm ways to improve your design

title: Mechanical Engineering: Crane
description: Learn about simple machines and how they work together as you build your own heavy-lifting crane. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to build and test a crane and understand simple and compound machines.

requirements.title: Brainstorm ways to improve your design

title: Mechanical Engineering: Paddle Boat
description: Explore how paddle boats work and engineer your own paddle boat. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to build and test a paddle boat and understand buoyancy, potential energy, and kinetic energy.

requirements.title: Design and build a rubber band-powered paddle boat
Test your rubber band-powered paddle boat
Brainstorm ways to improve your design

title: Musician
description: Find out more about the fun of making music. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how music is made and how to perform music yourself. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Digital Photographer, Staying Fit, Musician, Entertainment Technology, and Scribe badges.

requirements.title: Travel around the world of music
Check out the music in your life

title: My Cookie Team
description: Bring your different skills and ideas together to meet your goals. When you've earned this badge, you will know how to use teamwork to help your cookie business

requirements.title: Find out about Girl Scout Cookies
Decide how you will use your cookie money
Set a troop budget and package goal

title: My Money Plan
description: Find out how people earn money and make it grow by saving and investing. Then investigate ways to protect your money and information. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to earn an income, invest it, and protect it.

requirements.title: Explore how to earn an income
See the difference between saving and investing

title: Outdoor Art Explorer
description: Let nature be your inspiration as you explore, create, and design different kinds of art. When you've earned this badge, you will know how to find the art in nature and create your own outdoor artwork.

title: Playing the Past
description: Explore what it was like to live in the past. Dream up a character and get ready to live history! When you've earned this badge, you'll know how girls of the past lived, worked, and played. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Geocacher, Animal Habitats, Playing the Past, Product Designer, and Social Butterfly badges.

title: Practice with Purpose
description: Have fun with athletics as you learn to improve a skill. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to set a sports goal and practice to achieve it.

title: Robotics 1: Designing Robots
description: Plan and build a prototype of a robot that solves a global problem. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to plan, build, and share feedback like an engineer by creating a prototype of a robot that solves a global problem.

title: Robotics 2: Programming Robots
description: Engineer a simple machine that helps a robot land, learn about the robot brain, and create programs for your friends. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how robots receive instructions in a way similar to the human brain. You will be able to create simple programs that could be run by a robot.

title: Robotics 3: Showcasing Robots
description: Now that you have your robot prototype, create a presentation and share your design with others. Then, find out about robot teams and competitions.

requirements.title: Create a presentation to share how you designed your robot
Tell others how you designed your robot

title: Scribe
description: Explore what you can do with words and find out how you can encourage, entertain, and excite people with your writing. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to write different kinds of stories- both true tales and ideas from your imagination. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Digital Photographer, Staying Fit, Musician, Entertainment Technology, and Scribe badges.

requirements.title: Use words to share who you are
Tell the world what you think

title: Simple Meals
description: Make amazing meals and learn to create a whole meal of your own. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to serve up a meal for your family and friends.

requirements.title: Step up your skills with a pro
Fix a healthy lunch or dinner

title: Social Butterfly
description: Have fun practicing the social style and good manners that will make you comfortable in any situation. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to act at many types of events, from picnics to weddings to parties. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Geocacher, Animal Habitats, Playing the Past, Product Designer, and Social Butterfly badges.

title: Space Science Investigator
description: Venture through the Solar System and beyond, and see that space is even bigger than you may have imagined. When you've earned this badge, you'll understand that the Earth orbits the Sun, and how far away the Sun, Moon, planets, and stars are from our home planet, Earth.

title: Uniquely Us
topic: Health / Life Skills / Relationships / Mental Wellness
description: Discover how to appreciate what makes you one of a kind. When you've earned this badge, you'll understand that everyone is unique and we're all just right-including you!

requirements.title: Explore all the things your body can do
Know that it's not all about your body
Learn how to talk about it

title: Bronze Award
topic: Highest Awards / Leadership Awards / Life Skills / Leadership / Highest Award Prerequisite
description: The highest award you can earn as a Junior Girl Scout. By working as part of a team, explore a community issue and complete a project that addresses it.

requirements.title: Complete a Junior Leadership Award or Journey
Build your Junior Girl Scout team
Choose your Girl Scout Bronze Award project

title: Global Action
topic: Leadership Awards / Life Skills / Highest Award Prerequisite / Leadership / Relationships
description: Team up to make local change with a global community perspective. When you've earned this award, you'll know how to address a global issue with a sustainable and measurable solution.

title: Junior Aide
topic: Leadership Awards / Life Skills / Highest Award Prerequisite / Leadership / Relationships
description: Start building leadership skills by planning and leading an activity for younger kids. When you've earned this award, you'll know how to plan and lead a fun, inclusive activity for younger kids.

title: Junior True North Award
topic: Leadership Awards / Life Skills / Highest Award Prerequisite / Leadership
description: Discover what matters to you and build the confidence to act with courage. When you've earned this award, you will have explored what's important to you, built your confidence, and acted with courage.

requirements.title: Choose a goal that matters to you
Act with courage, confidence, and character

title: Take Action
topic: Leadership Awards / Life Skills / Leadership / Highest Award Prerequisite / Relationships
description: Team up to turn your passion into a lasting solution in your community. When you've earned this award, you'll know how to address a local community issue.

title: Animal Helper
description: Discover how animals help people-and discover how you can help animals in return. When you've earned this badge, you'll know more about the connections between people and animals, how animals help people, and what you can do to help animals.

requirements.title: Explore the connection between humans and animals
Find out how animals keep people safe
Find out how animals help people emotionally
Find out how animals perform tasks for people with disabilities
Find out how you can help animals

title: Archery
description: Find out about archery, an exciting sport that takes strength, focus, good form, and practice. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to take aim outdoors with your archery skills.

requirements.title: Practice archery before you go on a range

title: Babysitter
description: Gain the skills that will help you become the best babysitter you can be. When you've earned this badge, you'll be prepared to babysit and will have tried out your skills on the job. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Book Artist, Woodworker, Special Agent, Trailblazing, and Babysitter badges.

requirements.title: Get to know how kids develop

title: Book Artist
description: Learn book artristry. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to make different kinds of books. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Book Artist, Woodworker, Special Agent, Trailblazing, and Babysitter badges.

requirements.title: Get familiar with the insides of a book

title: Budget Manager
description: Look at your values, explore your money habits, and practice being a good budget manager. When you've earned this badge, you'll be able to make informed money decisions that reflect your values and know how to track your spending.

requirements.title: Find out how your budget reflects your values
Learn how to track your spending
Find ways to earn money for you or your troop
Explore how to make money decisions
Set a goal for how you'll give back

title: Business Creator
description: Become a business creator and learn the skills you need for success in life, no matter what future path you choose. When you've earned this badge, you will have learned to think like an entrepreneur when you come up with a business idea that solves a problem, design a prototype, solicit feedback, improve your idea, then create a business plan and pitch it.

requirements.title: Come up with an idea that solves a problem
Design a prototype and get feedback

title: Cadette First Aid
description: Learn how to deal with minor injuries and illnesses and how to recognize and respond to some more serious conditions. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to use everything in a first aid kit and how to respond to choking, seizures, and other health emergencies.

requirements.title: Know how to use everything in a first aid kit
Understand how to prevent and respond to choking emergencies
Find out how to handle outdoor musculoskeletal injuries
Learn how to recognize and respond to seizures
Learn to prevent and treat temperature-related health emergencies

title: Cadette Girl Scout Way
description: Learn about Girl Scout traditions and share activities and spirit that are a century strong. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to use Girl Scout ways and traditions to make the world a better place.

requirements.title: Share sisterhood through the Girl Scout Law
Leave a camp better than you found it

title: Cadette STEM Career Exploration
description: Explore careers that make a difference. Then, map out how you'll help others and change the world! When you've earned this badge, you'll know about STEM careers and how you can make the world a better place.

requirements.title: Explore how you want to make a difference

title: Cadette Snow or Climbing Adventure
description: Choose your adventure: Go on a skiing/snowboarding trip, or have an outdoor climbing adventure. When you've earned this badge, you'll have planned, learned the skills, and experienced a snowboarding or ski trip or an outdoor climbing adventure.

requirements.title: Set a goal and train for your adventure

title: Cadette Trail Adventure
description: Choose your adventure: Go on a long-distance trail run, or take a hiking challenge. When you've earned this badge, you'll have planned and learned the skills to do a long-distance trail run or take three separate challenging trail hikes.

requirements.title: Set a goal and train for your adventure

title: Coding for Good 1: Coding Basics
description: Find out how to write a computer program and create a meme with a positive message. When you've earned this badge, you'll know the basics of computer coding and how to create a meme that can have an impact on other people, your community, and the world.

title: Coding for Good 2: Digital Game Design
description: Explore how programmers design video games that make positive changes in the world. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how video games are developed, how images are stored and displayed on screens, and how to use elements from Javascript to create game characters.

requirements.title: Learn how to use arrays to create images
Write an array to create an icon

title: Coding for Good 3: App Development
description: Collect and visualize personal data to design a protype for an app that will help to build a healthy habit. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to use elements from JavaScript to create your own idea for a habit tracking app.

requirements.title: Learn about data collection and visualization
Write an array to store personal data
Create a personal data collection plan
Develop a prototype for a habit tracking app

title: Comic Artist
description: Find out more about the world of comic art and then tell your own visual story. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to create your own comic.

requirements.title: Delve into the world of comics

title: Cookie Entrepreneur Family Pin
description: Take the lead-getting support from your family-as you earn this award while running your Girl Scout Cookie® business.

title: Cookie Entrepreneur Family Pin
description: Take the lead-getting support from your family-as you earn this award while running your Girl Scout Cookie® business.

title: Cookie Entrepreneur Family Pin
description: Take the lead-getting support from your family-as you earn this award while running your Girl Scout Cookie® business.

title: Cookie Innovator
description: Create bolder and bigger goals for your cookie business and find unique ways to connect with your customers. When you've earned this badge, you will know how to set bigger goals for your cookie business and come up with ways to reach them.

requirements.title: Choose a big idea, set goals, and build a budget
Decide how to meet your big goals
Explore ethics in your cookie business
Sell your big dream to others

title: Cookie Market Researcher
description: Explore your customers' experience to help you reach more people and expand your business. When you've earned this badge, you will know how to use market research to grow your cookie business and connect with customers.

requirements.title: Explore the Girl Scout Cookie brand

title: Cybersecurity 1: Basics
description: Explore different hacking techniques and how to protect yourself with basic cybersecurity precautions. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how (and why it's important!) to protect yourself with strong passwords, secure encryption, and caution when clicking on links.

title: Cybersecurity 2: Safeguards
description: Find out why it's important to keep your personal information private online and how you can keep your data secure. When you've earned this badge, you'll know about your digital footprint and how others can use data from your footprint to target you with both legitimate ads and social engineering scams.

requirements.title: Create a profile based on your interests
Shop for apps in a life-sized app store

title: Cybersecurity 3: Investigator
description: Solve a series of fictional cyber crimes using what you know about cybersecurity. When you've earned this badge, you'll know about the potential vulnerabilities and risks in personal digital communications (emails, text messages, and social media) and how you can protect your identity.

requirements.title: Learn how hackers use social media

title: Democracy for Cadettes
description: If you want to make a difference, start by fully understanding how your government works. Informed citizens are change makers! When you've earned this badge, you will have learned new things about your government-how each branch works, how they work together, and how each piece makes a difference in society.

requirements.title: Find out about the federal legislative branch
Find out about the federal executive branch
Find out about the federal judicial branch

title: Digital Leadership
description: Explore the digital world and how you can use technology to inspire and guide others. When you've earned this badge, you will know how technology can be used to make a difference. You'll know how to make the internet a better place for yourself and others.

title: Digital Movie Maker
description: Try your hand at movie making. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to create a digital movie. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Digital Movie Maker, Eating for You, Public Speaker, Science of Happiness, and Screenwriter badges.

title: Eco Trekker
description: Plan and take an outdoor trek-with minimal environmental impact. When you've earned this badge, you'll have learned the skills for minimizing your impact on the environment while planning and taking an outdoor trek.

requirements.title: Learn how to make minimal impact on a trek
Plan an eco trek with a purpose
Practice an eco skill while on your trek
Make a difference on your eco trek
Make a difference after your eco trek

title: Field Day
description: From friends playing in the park to countries competing against one another at the Olympics, games unite us all. And Girl Scouts have been inventing their own games since 1912! In this badge, you'll experience this firsthand, by organizing teams and events for an exciting, do-it-yourself field day. When I've earned this badge, I'll know how to organize a field day filled with friends and fun.

title: Finding Common Ground
description: Find out how people in democratic governments make trade-offs, giving up some things they favor to gain support for others. When you've earned this badge, you'll know strategies for bringing people together to find common ground.

requirements.title: Get to know someone different from you

title: Good Sportsmanship
description: Find out how to make good sportsmanship a habit in games and in life. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how you define sportsmanship and will have put your definition into action.

requirements.title: Create your own definition of sportsmanship
Put your definition of good sportsmanship into action

title: Journey: Breathe
description: In this Journey, you will:

requirements.title: Find out about the air we breathe by visiting a wind farm, talking to an environmental scientist, or doing experiments using wind.
Plan a Take Action project to fix an air problem in your community. You could plant an indoor garden, campaign against smoking, or push for clean-air initiatives in your community.
Earn 3 leadership awards: Aware, Alert, and Affirm. If you're a Girl Scout volunteer, go to Volunteer Toolkit for complete meeting plans and activity instructions. More Details

title: Journey: MEdia
description: In this Journey, you will:

requirements.title: Explore media and find out how to reshape negative media messages into more positive ones-invite a TV show producer to talk about what influences her work, organize a movie night and discuss the film's messages, or write a rap song or TV script.

Plan a Take Action Project that encourages positive messages in media-rewrite a song that has hurtful lyrics about women, create a blog that reviews how movies and TV shows depict girls, or start a Twitter campaign responding to stereotypes you see in the media.

title: Journey: Outdoor (Cadette)
description: In this Journey, you will:

requirements.title: Deepen your outdoor skills when you earn your Night Owl, Trailblazing, and Primitive Camper badges.
Plan a Take Action project that helps make your favorite park, beach, or forest a better place for everyone.

Earn a Take Action Award. If you're a Girl Scout volunteer, go to Volunteer Toolkit for complete meeting plans and activity instructions. Learn more about how to earn your Take Action Award - and help your community - with the Girl Scout Take Action Guide. Then use your leadership skills to earn your Silver Award, the highest award for Girl Scout Cadettes!

title: Journey: Think Like a Citizen Scientist
description: In this Journey, you will:

requirements.title: Find out how citizen scientists make observations, collect data, and help scientists conduct scientific research.
Do 3 citizen science activities: make observations about your environment, create a community field guide, and a SciStarter project.
Plan a Take Action project that helps others. If you're a Girl Scout volunteer, go to Volunteer Toolkit for complete meeting plans and activity instructions.

title: Journey: Think Like a Programmer
description: In this Journey, you will:

requirements.title: Find out how programmers use computational thinking to solve problems

Do 3 computational thinking activities: categorize various objects as "Computer" or "Not a Computer" to explore the four tasks that define a computer, create a card- sorting algorithm to learn about processing, and design technology that willl best meet a user's needs using user-centered design.
Plan a Take Action project that helps others. If you're a Girl Scout volunteer, go to Volunteer Toolkit for complete meeting plans and activity instructions.

title: Journey: Think Like an Engineer
description: In this Journey, you will:

requirements.title: Find out how engineers use design thinking to solve problems.
Do 3 design thinking activities: design and build prototypes of a life vest for a dog, a model camp cabin inspired by nature, and a prosthetic leg for an elephant.

Plan a Take Action project that helps others. Learn more about how to earn your Take Action Award - and help your community - with the Girl Scout Take Action Guide. Then use your leadership skills to earn your Silver Award, the highest award for Girl Scout Cadettes!

title: Journey: aMAZE!
description: In this Journey, you will:

requirements.title: Find out how to develop true friendships, navigate cliques, and move beyond stereotypes, with the ultimate goal of peace in your interpersonal relationships.
Plan a Take Action Project that shares your new friendship-building skills.

Earn 3 leadership awards: Interact, Diplomat, and Peacemaker. If you're a Girl Scout volunteer, go to Volunteer Toolkit for complete meeting plans and activity instructions. Learn more about how to earn your Take Action Award - and help your community - with the Girl Scout Take Action Guide. Then use your leadership skills to earn your Silver Award, the highest award for Girl Scout Cadettes!

title: My Cookie Venture
description: Learn to expand your knowledge of your business resources and customers and create a business plan that will guide you to your most successful cookie season yet. When you've earned this badge, you will know how to create a business plan for your cookie business.

requirements.title: Get prepared for your cookie business

title: My Dream Budget
description: Create a budget to support your 25-year-old self. When I've earned this badge, you'll be able to create a budget for your future life.

requirements.title: Make a plan to give back

title: My Money Habits
description: Develop positive habits that help you reach your goals and protect your finances. When you've earned this badge, you'll learn what it means to invest, manage your spending habits, and make smart money decisions.

requirements.title: Investigate how to be a safe and savvy shopper

title: Netiquette
description: Our digital world makes it easy to stay in touch and share friendships, memories, and ideas. But we've all heard embarrassing stories in which people wish they'd never hit the "send" button. Once you've put it out there, it never goes away. But you can use that to your benefit, too. This badge is all about knowing how to make positive choices in the online world. When I've earned this badge, I'll know how to make -- and keep -- my online world a positive place.

requirements.title: Dig into stories of "ouch" -- and repair some hurt if necessary
Look at e-mail, commenting, or blogging
Decide what makes a great social media profile

title: New Cuisines
description: Cook up four dishes from across time and distance, and find out where your taste buds want to travel. When you've earned this badge, you'll be able to make amazing dishes from all over the world and way back in time.

requirements.title: Make a dish from another country
Create a dish from another region of the United States
Whip up a dish from another time period
Cook a dish that makes a statement
Share your dishes on a culinary "tour"!

title: Night Owl
description: Grow your imagination and expand what you know about the night. When you've earned this badge, you'll have uncovered the mysteries of the world after dark. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Night Owl, Animal Helpers, Field Day, Entrepreneur, and Netiquette badges.

requirements.title: Take a field trip to explore the night
Meet people who work night hours

title: Outdoor Art Apprentice
description: Find out how to observe and collect things outdoors that will drive your art and creativity. When you've earned this badge, you'll have been inspired outdoors and created nature-themed art.

requirements.title: Get to know-and create-sounds of nature

title: Outside the Mirror
topic: Health / Life Skills / Relationships / Mental Wellness
description: Find out how to look beyond the mirror and define beauty for yourself. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to push back against hidden beauty standard messaging and appreciate your authentic self.

requirements.title: Look to the past to understand the present
Think about how images and looks can be altered
Be on the lookout for hidden messages

title: Primitive Camper
description: Learn to tackle outdoor activities without the comforts of home. When you've earned this badge, you'll have planned and gone on a primitive camping trip with a group of Girl Scouts or family members.

requirements.title: Learn a new primitive camping skill

title: Public Speaker
description: Work on your stage presence and develop your onstage skills. When you've earned this badge, you'll have found your inner performer and be unafraid to speak up. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Digital Movie Maker, Eating for You, Public Speaker, Science of Happiness, and Screenwriter badges.

requirements.title: Get a feel for performing solo
Choose or create a piece to perform

title: Robotics 1: Programming Robots
description: To help you understand how robots work, learn about the parts that make up a robot. Get started with electronics by making a simple sensor, something robots use to function without human operators. Then practice coding robots using important programming concepts like functions and loops. When you've earned this badge, you'll understand how robots work and how to control them.

requirements.title: Build a robot part: simple sensors
Make a box model robot with sensors
Write a program for a robot

title: Robotics 2: Designing Robots
description: Build a prototype of a new kind of robot that could help someone to overcome a daily obstacle. Decide on a challenge, brainstorm solutions, plan, build a prototype, and test it to see how well it meets the challenge. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to design a robot and build a prototype.

title: Robotics 3: Showcasing Robots
description: After engineers build a working version of their robots, they release them to the public. They may enter them into challenges and competitions or market them through a website or crowd-funding campaign. Now that you've built your robot, share your design with others and explore a future in robotics. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to share your robot designs with the world.

requirements.title: Learn about robotics events and organizations
Create a presentation about your robot
Present your robot pitch to others for feedback
Find out about robotics opportunities for teens
See robot makers and robots in action

title: Science of Happiness
description: Find out how scientists measure happiness and put their results into action. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to use the science of happiness to make your world the happiest place it can be. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Digital Movie Maker, Eating for You, Public Speaker, Science of Happiness, and Screenwriter badges

title: Screenwriter
description: Try your hand at writing for the big (or small) screen. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to create a screenplay for a show or movie. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Digital Movie Maker, Eating for You, Public Speaker, Science of Happiness, and Screenwriter badges.

requirements.title: Decide what makes a good script good
Come up with an idea for a story

title: Space Science Researcher
description: Observe and explore light, deepening your understanding of the Sun, stars, and other objects in space. When you've earned this badge, you'll understand more about the amazing properties of light and how you use it to make discoveries about the Universe and space science.

requirements.title: See the stars in a new way

title: Special Agent
description: Use your investigative mind to take on the many roles of a special agent: detective, scientist, psychologist, enforcer of the law. When you've earned this badge, you'll know secrets from the worlds of forensic science and criminal psychology. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Book Artist, Woodworker, Special Agent, Trailblazing, and Babysitter badges.

title: Trailblazing
description: Gain new outdoor skills when you stay at least one night at a backwoods or primitive site. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to take a safe and fun overnight backpacking trip. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Book Artist, Woodworker, Special Agent, Trailblazing, and Babysitter badges.

requirements.title: Get your body and your teamwork skills ready

title: Trees
description: Find out all about trees: from the shade to the science, the fruit to the forest, and the legends to the lumber. When you've earned this badge, you'll have gone to the root of what trees are all about-and branched out as a naturalist.

requirements.title: Dig into the amazing science of trees
Make a creative project starring trees
Explore the connection between people and trees

title: Woodworker
description: Find out how to use wood to make stylish, useful things for yourself and others. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to use some basic tools to make simple woodworking projects. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Book Artist, Woodworker, Special Agent, Trailblazing, and Babysitter badges.

title: Cadette True North Award
topic: Leadership Awards / Life Skills / Leadership / Highest Award Prerequisite
description: Discover what matters to you and how those things shape your choices, then build the confidence to act with courage. When you've earned this award, you will have explored what's important to you, built your confidence, and acted with courage.

requirements.title: Choose a goal that matters to you
Act with courage, confidence, and character

title: Global Action
topic: Leadership Awards / Life Skills / Highest Award Prerequisite / Leadership / Relationships
description: Team up to make local change with a global community perspective. When you've earned this award, you'll know how to address a global issue with a sustainable and measurable solution.

title: Program Aide
topic: Leadership Awards / Life Skills / Leadership / Highest Award Prerequisite / Relationships
description: Explore your personal leadership style as you plan activities and lead a meeting for younger kids. When you've earned this award, you'll have planned and led multiple activities for a group of younger kids.

title: Silver Award
topic: Highest Awards / Life Skills / Leadership / Highest Award Prerequisite
description: The highest award you can earn as a Cadette Girl Scout. By working as an individual or part of a small team, research an issue, make a plan to address it, and then take action to improve your community. Prerequisite: Complete the Bronze Award, a Cadette Leadership Award, or a Journey

requirements.title: Make a plan and submit your project proposal
Spread the word and submit your final report

title: Silver Torch
topic: Leadership Awards / Life Skills / Leadership / Highest Award Prerequisite / Relationships
description: Learn how to shine as a leader while working with a team. When you've earned this award, you'll know how to lead by being a great teammate.

title: Take Action
topic: Leadership Awards / Life Skills / Leadership / Highest Award Prerequisite / Relationships
description: Team up to turn your passion into a lasting solution in your community. When you've earned this award, you'll know how to address a local community issue.

title: Automotive 1: Design
description: Find out how automotive designers come up with ideas for new vehicles. Then, take the wheel and design your own! When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to design, sketch, and sculpt a vehicle.

requirements.title: Explore how people move from place to place
Sketch a vehicle to meet your criteria

title: Automotive 2: Engineering
description: Get in gear and explore how automotive engineers build vehicles. Then, engineer your own model of a vehicle to help people during an emergency! When you've earned this badge, you'll know how automotive engineers plan, build, test, and improve vehicles.

requirements.title: Learn about simple machines in vehicles
Engineer a vehicle for safety based on criteria
Test and revise your vehicle prototype
Share your vehicle prototype and testing results

title: Automotive 3: Manufacturing
description: Experience the importance of having a plan and discover how manufacturers build lots of vehicles that are safe and well made! When you've earned this badge, you'll know how vehicles are manufactured in a factory. You'll also know how to make and test products for people to buy.

requirements.title: Learn about the automotive manufacturing process
Plan your own automotive manufacturing process

title: Brownie Art and Design
description: Discover different styles and elements of art and design. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how makers turn ideas into art and design projects.

title: Brownie Craft and Tinker
description: Discover how to be a problem solver by making or crafting solutions. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to craft and tinker to solve a problem.

title: Brownie Create and Innovate
description: Discover how makers turn ideas into objects, improve them, and make the world better. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to create objects that help others.

title: Brownie First Aid
description: Find out how to get help in an emergency and treat minor injuries so you can be prepared if the unexpected happens. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to get help in an emergency and treat minor injuries.

requirements.title: Find out how to get help from 911
Talk to someone who treats injured people
Learn how to treat minor injuries
Know how to prevent and treat outdoor injuries

title: Brownie Girl Scout Way
description: Find out how you can share the Girl Scout legacy. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to practice and share the Girl Scout way.

requirements.title: Leave a place better than you found it

title: Brownie STEM Career Exploration
description: Find out how all the things you love-like camping, baking, and walking your dog-can become a job that changes the world. Then, create a plan to turn your dreams into a reality! When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to use STEM to help others.

title: Brownie Snow or Climbing Adventure
description: Choose your adventure: Ski along trails on a cross-country winter adventure, or learn climbing skills on an artificial wall, indoors or outdoors. When you've earned this badge, you'll have planned and learned the skills for cross- country skiing or rock climbing on a climbing wall.

title: Brownie Trail Adventure
description: Choose your adventure: Go trail running, or take different kinds of hikes. When you've earned this badge, you'll have planned and learned the skills for an outdoor trail run or three different types of hikes.

title: Budding Entrepreneur
description: Become an entrepreneur by making something, testing it out, and improving your idea to make it the best it can be! When you've earned this badge, you will have come up with an idea for something that solves a problem, gotten feedback on it, and pitched it to others-all things that entrepreneurs do!

requirements.title: Come up with an idea that makes life better
Share your idea with others to get feedback
Pitch your idea to an audience

title: Budget Builder
description: Find out what it means to spend and save and how to build a budget. When you've earned this badge, you will know how to make the shopping choices and budget decisions that are best for yourself.

requirements.title: Find out how much things cost and how money is used
Learn the difference between wants and needs
Plan a budget for a fun adventure
Find out how to be thrifty
Make a budget for your character

title: Bugs
description: Explore the world of bugs and learn more about these little creatures that do so much. When you've earned this badge, you'll know all about bugs.

title: Cabin Camper
description: Plan a camping adventure with your Girl Scout friends or family members and then go camping! When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to plan a camping trip and stay overnight in a cabin* with your friends or family. *If you don't have a cabin to use, that's ok! You can sleep in a tent instead.

title: Celebrating Community
description: Learn about community celebrations and find out what your communities do to celebrate all the people in them. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how your community celebrates what makes it special.

title: Coding for Good 1: Coding Basics
description: Find out how computer scientists write programs for computers to solve problems. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how programmers write programs that make computers work and how people can use computers to help others.

requirements.title: Create algorithms for a computer that follow a sequence
Use loops to improve your algorithm
Use events to make things happen
Learn about women in computer science
Create your own set of commands that use events

title: Coding for Good 2: Digital Game Design
description: Explore how video games can help people to learn new skills and experience new things. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to think like a game maker and use iteration to plan, build, and test a game.

requirements.title: Discover how game design can be used "for good"
Explore tools used to develop digital games
Build, test, and improve your maze game using iteration

title: Coding for Good 3: App Development
description: Design your own idea for an app that solves a problem for someone else. When you've earned this badge, you'll know about user-centered design and how computer scientists develop apps.

requirements.title: Discover how apps can be used for good
Decompose the needs of your app user
Create algorithms for your app that include events
Share and improve your app with user feedback

title: Computer Expert
description: Find out what computers can help you do and learn to be a safe, secure computer expert. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to do many useful things on a computer. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Computer Expert, My Best Self, Dancer, Home Scientist, and My Family Story badges.

requirements.title: Paint or draw with an art program

title: Cookie Decision Maker
description: Learn to think like an entrepreneur by making decisions for your cookie business. When you've earned this badge, you will know how to make decisions that help your business succeed.

requirements.title: Get to know your Girl Scout Cookie business
Practice handling money and tracking sales

title: Cookie Entrepreneur Family Pin
description: Have fun with your family as you earn this award while running your Girl Scout Cookie® business.

title: Cookie Entrepreneur Family Pin
description: Have fun with your family as you earn this award while running your Girl Scout Cookie® business.

title: Cybersecurity 1: Basics
description: Find out how you use technology and how you can keep your technology safe. When you've earned this badge, you'll know cybersecurity basics and understand the role technology plays in your life.

requirements.title: Find out how you use technology
Discover what your technology can do
Find out how to create layers of security
Find out how to use real-life safety rules when you go online
Find out how messages travel on the internet

title: Cybersecurity 2: Safeguards
description: Find out how to be safe when you go online. When you've earned this badge, you'll know what information is private and how to share information safely.

requirements.title: Find out what information to keep private when you go online
Find out how to share information safely online
Find out why you have to be careful about who you trust online
Test your knowledge of online safety rules

title: Cybersecurity 3: Investigator
description: Put on your detective hat and solve cyber crimes. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to use investigative skills to spot problems in the cyber world.

requirements.title: Crack a code to solve a problem
Investigate what's real and fake in photos
Investigate how a computer virus can spread

title: Dancer
description: Learn fun warm-ups, perform steps from new dances, and make a dance of your very own. When you've earned this badge, you'll have explored the world of dancing-and found the dancer inside of you. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Computer Expert, My Best Self, Dancer, Home Scientist, and My Family Story badges.

requirements.title: Take to the floor like a dancer

title: Democracy for Brownies
description: Find out how people in the government decide what rules are made and how they are followed. When you've earned this badge, you will know the three branches of government-and some of the things they are responsible for.

requirements.title: Find out about our country's legislative branch
Find out about our country's executive branch
Find out about our country's judicial branch

title: Digital Leadership
description: Find out how technology can help you to be a leader who teaches, inspires, and makes the real and digital worlds a better place. When you've earned this badge, you will know how to lead in the digital world. You'll know how to use technology to make the internet and world a better place.

title: Eco Friend
description: Find out how to treat outdoor spaces with kindness and teach others how they can, too. When you've earned this badge, you'll have found new ways to protect our natural world and the living things in it.

requirements.title: Think of ways to help the outdoors

title: Fair Play
description: Playing fair means that everyone has the same chance to play, because everyone follows the same rules. That's what this badge is about. You'll learn to work together to have the most fun possible. That's fair play! When I've earned this badge, I'll know how to play fair in sports.

title: Hiker
description: Find out what you need to know to become a Brownie hiker. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to hit the trail for a hike. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Potter, Household Elf, Senses, Hiker, and My Great Day badges.

title: Home Scientist
description: Find out where science has been hiding in your home. When you've earned this badge, you'll be able to see the science all around you. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Computer Expert, My Best Self, Dancer, Home Scientist, and My Family Story badges.

title: Household Elf
description: Pick up some new household habits to help your family save energy, save water, and save the planet. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to make your home clean and green. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Potter, Household Elf, Senses, Hiker, and My Great Day badges.

title: Journey: A World of Girls
description: In this Journey, you will:

requirements.title: Find out about girls around the world and how stories can give you ideas for helping others-play a game from another culture, invite storytellers to talk about what they do, or draw a self-portrait that shows what's unique about you.
Plan a Take Action Project, such as sewing a quilt, painting a mural, writing a play, or creating your own ad.

title: Journey: Brownie Quest
description: In this Journey, you will:

requirements.title: Find out how to take care of yourself, your family, your Girl Scout sisters, and your community-do an activity with your family to chart everyone's special talents, play games at your troop meetings to learn how to work together, or do a fun "clue hunting" activity to learn the Girl Scout Law.

Plan a Take Action project, such as visiting a nursery school to read to younger children or teach them a game, creating a school skit about healthy eating, or making a presentation to your city council about fixing a broken sidewalk by your school.

title: Journey: Outdoor (Brownie)
description: In this Journey, you will:

requirements.title: Deepen your outdoor skills when you earn your Brownie First Aid, Hiker badger, and Cabin Camper badges.
Plan a Take Action project that helps others.

Earn a Take Action award. If you're a Girl Scout volunteer, go to Volunteer Toolkit for complete meeting plans and activity instructions. Learn more about how to earn your Take Action Award - and help your community - with the Girl Scout Take Action Guide.

title: Journey: Think Like a Citizen Scientist
description: In this Journey, you will:

requirements.title: Find out how citizen scientists make observations, collect data, and work with scientists to receive feedback on research.
Do 3 citizen science activities: sharpen your observation skills through 2 observation games and a SciStarter project.

Plan a Take Action project that helps others. If you're a Girl Scout volunteer, go to Volunteer Toolkit for complete meeting plans and activity instructions. Learn more about how to earn your Take Action Award - and help your community - with the Girl Scout Take Action Guide.

title: Journey: Think Like a Programmer
description: In this Journey, you will:

requirements.title: Find out how programmers use computational thinking to solve problems.

Do 3 computational thinking activities: find out about paper programming; create a functional suncatcher to explore algorithms, variables, and functions; and create a personal innovation to discover rapid prototyping.

Plan a Take Action project that helps others. If you're a Girl Scout volunteer, go to Volunteer Toolkit for complete meeting plans and activity instructions. Learn more about how to earn your Take Action Award - and help your community - with the Girl Scout Take Action Guide.

title: Journey: Think Like an Engineer
description: In this Journey, you will:

requirements.title: Find out how engineers use design thinking to solve problems.
Do 3 design thinking activities: design and build an assistive device, a water collection device, and a device that can launch a ball across a room.

Plan a Take Action project that helps others. If you're a Girl Scout volunteer, go to Volunteer Toolkit for complete meeting plans and activity instructions. Learn more about how to earn your Take Action Award - and help your community - with the Girl Scout Take Action Guide.

title: Journey: WOW! Wonders of Water
description: In this Journey, you will:

requirements.title: Explore the wonders of water by find out more about its importance and how it's used around the world.
Plan a Take Action project, such as making informative posters, promoting recycling at school, or planting low-water gardens.

Earn 4 leadership awards: The LOVE Water Award, the SAVE Water Award, the SHARE Water Award, and the WOW! Award. If you're a Girl Scout volunteer, go to Volunteer Toolkit for complete meeting plans and activity instructions.

title: Letterboxer
description: Learn about letterboxing, an amazing adventure game played by people all over the world. To play, you'll follow clues to find a hidden box with special items inside. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to have fun adventures by finding and hiding letterboxes. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Letterboxer, Pets, Making Games, Inventor, and Making Friends badges.

title: Making Friends
description: Find out how to make new friends, keep old friends, and be the best Brownie friend you can be. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to be a good friend. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Letterboxer, Pets, Making Games, Inventor, and Making Friends badges.

title: Making Games
description: Use your imagination to make up new games. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to create new games and share them with others. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Letterboxer, Pets, Making Games, Inventor, and Making Friends badges.

title: Math in Nature 1: Shapes in Nature
description: Look carefully at nature, track what you see, and create art inspired by natural objects. When you've earned this badge, you will know about counting, graphing, symmetry, and tessellations. You'll also know about spiders, birds, and other natural objects.

title: Math in Nature 2: Numbers in Nature
description: Use your senses and other tools to gather information and learn about the natural world. When you've earned this badge, you will know how to measure temperature and length. You'll also know about square feet, diagrams, and grids. You'll have explored leaves and gardening.

title: Math in Nature 3: Design with Nature
description: Use math to do things in the natural world, like find the age of natural objects, build a honeycomb, design a bird feeder, and go bird-watching. When you've earned this badge, you will know about natural objects. You'll know how to measure, use scale and ratio, and make a graph.

requirements.title: Calculate the age of a natural object
Measure and build a bird feeder
Use ratios to make bird food

title: Me Time
topic: Health / Life Skills / Mental Wellness / Relationships
description: Find out how to care for yourself and feel your best. When you've earned this badge, you'll know what you can do to feel your best, both mentally and physically.

title: Mechanical Engineering: Fling Flyer
description: Work like an engineer to create a Fling Flyer, an airplane you'll make, and explore what keeps it and other things, such as birds, planes, and space ships, in the air. When you've earned this badge, you'll have learned about the forces that affect flight as you design, build, and test a Fling Flyer. You'll know how to design an investigation- and fine-tune your design after testing it, just like engineers.

requirements.title: Learn about forces that affect flight
Design and build a Fling Flyer
Brainstorm ways to improve your design

title: Mechanical Engineering: Leap Bot
description: Design, build, and test things like an engineer as you create your own Leap Bot. When you've earned this badge, you'll have learned about engineering, gravity, and force by building and testing a Leap Bot. You will know how to build and test a new product.

requirements.title: Create a way to test how well your Leap Bot performs
Record the results of your test

title: Mechanical Engineering: Race Car
description: Design, build, and test your own race car to explore how science can make a faster race car! When you've earned this badge, you'll have designed a race car and a race track and carried out "fair tests" to learn how design affects speed.

requirements.title: Learn how design can affect speed
Design and build your race car
Conduct a fair test and record results

title: My Cookie Customers
description: Find out how to get new customers and keep them coming back. When you've earned this badge, you will know how to find new customers and talk to them about your cookie business.

requirements.title: Decide how to use your cookie money

title: My Family Story
description: Find out more about your family-the people you love and who love you. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to tell your family story. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Computer Expert, My Best Self, Dancer, Home Scientist, and My Family Story badges.

requirements.title: Know where your family is from
Find an object that means something to your family

title: My Great Day
description: Try out ways to get organized and learn how to help your family and friends stay organized too. When you've earned this badge, you'll be able to organize yourself to have a great day every day. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Potter, Household Elf, Senses, Hiker, and My Great Day badges.

title: My Own Budget
description: Find out how to spend, save, and share by creating a budget. When you've earned this badge, you will know how to spend, save, and share money.

requirements.title: Find out how people spend money
Find out what it means to share
Make a budget and set goals.

title: Outdoor Adventurer
description: Explore outdoor activities that are new to you. When you've earned this badge, you'll have tried a lot of new things outdoors and learned which activities you want to do again.

requirements.title: Play outdoors in a new way
Buddy up and play outdoor games

title: Outdoor Art Creator
description: Find out how you can make your own art outdoors and have fun doing it. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to do different kinds of fun art projects outdoors.

title: Pets
description: Learn how to choose the pet that's right for you-or find out how to take the best care of a pet you already have. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to take care of a pet.

requirements.title: Find out what care different pets need
Help a pet stay healthy and safe

title: Robotics 1: Designing Robots
description: Team up with your fellow Brownies to design a robot. Plan, build, and share your robot prototype. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to plan, build, and share feedback like an engineer by creating a prototype of a robot that helps other people or animals.

requirements.title: Learn about the parts of a robot

title: Robotics 2: Programming Robots
description: Learn about the robot brain by engineering a machine that helps a robot to land When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to create a program that could be run by a robot.

title: Robotics 3: Showcasing Robots
description: After engineers build their robots, they show them to others and enter them into challenges and competitions. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to share your robot with others.

requirements.title: Create a presentation to share how you designed your robot
Tell others how you designed your robot
See robots in action Note: Unlike the other Robotics badges, these steps include options. You may be able to complete multiple steps, particularly three through five, at once.

title: Senses
description: You have five senses that work together to let you see, hear, smell, taste, and touch the world around you. Try all five in this badge! When you've earned this badge, you'll know how you use your five senses to explore the world. This badge comes in a packet that includes the Potter, Household Elf, Senses, Hiker, and My Great Day badges.

title: Snacks
description: Make great snacks for yourself and your friends. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to make yummy snacks for your family and friends.

requirements.title: Find out about different types of snacks

title: Space Science Adventurer
description: Investigate the complexities of the sky as you learn to see things in a new way. When you've earned this badge, you'll know how to investigate the Sun, Moon, planets, and stars.
